root_cause_synthesis.most_likely_scenario: STALE BOT PROCESS + OUTDATED .env + OLD PYCACHE
mechanism: Apr 10 23:59: Main bot process started with MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.65 in .env
Apr 11 00:02: Main bot process crashed or was killed by launcher

Apr 11 00:00-00:37: A STALE BACKGROUND PROCESS (possibly started BEFORE Apr 10 23:59 restart) continued running with OLD .env that did NOT have MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE set to 0.65 (or had it set to default 0.60 or lower)
Apr 11 00:00-00:37: This stale process opened trades 241/243/244 using OLD pycache (especially 4-day-old model_bridge bytecode) without proper confidence gate or with outdated gate threshold
Apr 11 10:46: Someone manually corrected .env to set MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.65 explicitly
Apr 11 10:57: New clean bot process started with corrected .env
root_cause_synthesis.why_no_logs_during_00_00_37: The stale process was either logging to a different file, or was a detached daemon process not capturing output, or was the launcher process itself rather than the main bot process

explicit_rulings.stale_bytecode_plausible: YES — model_bridge.cpython-314.pyc is 4 days old (Apr 07). Could contain old gate logic or no gate at all.
explicit_rulings.wrong_process_likely: YES — strong evidence of two concurrent processes. Main process exited at 00:02. Stale process continued.
explicit_rulings.wrong_python_path: UNKNOWN — both startups used same interpreter path
explicit_rulings.wrong_working_tree: UNKNOWN — cwd and env_path match current state, but could be different at runtime if symlinks or env vars were involved
explicit_rulings.wrong_env_config_at_time: ALMOST CERTAIN — .env was modified at 10:46 UTC, AFTER trades opened. The prior .env at 00:00-00:37 likely did NOT have MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE=0.65.

next_fix_targets: PRIORITY 1: Retrieve or reconstruct the .env file state at 2026-04-11 00:00 UTC (before modification at 10:46). What was MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE set to then?
PRIORITY 2: Check launch_all.py logs (bot_core_launcher.log) for process start/stop events between 23:59 Apr 10 and 10:57 Apr 11. Identify what process opened trades at 00:00-00:37 and why.
PRIORITY 3: Clear __pycache__ completely before each bot restart, or use -B flag to force recompilation of all .pyc files.
PRIORITY 4: Add process locking mechanism to ensure only one bot instance runs at a time (file lock or PID file check).
PRIORITY 5: Implement continuous logging to a separate file that captures ALL process activity, not just main bot loop output, to fill future gaps.

runtime_evidence_established.critical_finding_1: .env file mtime is 2026-04-11 10:46, AFTER trades opened at 00:00-00:37
runtime_evidence_established.critical_finding_2: 10+ hour logging gap during which trades opened with zero log entries

acceptance_criteria_met.clear_next_fix_recommendation: Retrieve prior .env state from backups/git history. Identify stale process in launcher logs. Implement single-process lock and .pyc cache clearing.
